roadmap.goal: Render a persistent left navigation on EVERY read-only viewer surface that stays mounted across navigation (content-only swap into an outer #viewer-main), marks the current surface active, and degrades to plain full-page links with JS off. Chrome-only, render-only, read-only, offline/loopback preserved, additive: no new crate/route/read-method (workspace stays 21). Mechanism per ADR-058 (D1-D6): outer <main id="viewer-main"> (VIEWER_MAIN_ID), persistent <nav id="viewer-nav"> with <ul id="viewer-nav-items"> sourced from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES, a page_shell(title, active, content) chrome helper every render_*_page routes through, hx-boost + hx-select="#viewer-main" nav links, a Shape::from_request HX-Boosted -> FullPage fork, and an out-of-band render_viewer_nav_oob(active) appended to boosted responses to update the active marker in place.

phases.name: Walking skeleton (nav on every surface) + single-source landing migration

steps.name: Thick walking skeleton: page_shell + render_viewer_nav + #viewer-main on every full page, current surface marked active, HX-Boosted -> FullPage fork
steps.description:
The thick foundational walking-skeleton step (NAV-1, the load-bearing demo thread of US-NAV-001/002). Force the WHOLE nav-chrome seam into existence end-to-end so the operator can reach every surface from every surface and always know where she is. PURE CORE (crates/viewer-domain/src/common.rs): mint VIEWER_MAIN_ID = "viewer-main" (SSOT for the outer swap target, sibling of VIEW_PANEL_ID / CLAIMS_TABLE_ID); mint render_viewer_nav(active: &str) -> Markup that iterates LANDING_HUB_SURFACES emitting one plain per surface inside
    ...
, the
carrying hx-boost="true" + hx-target="#viewer-main" + hx-select="#viewer-main" + hx-swap="innerHTML" (cascades to the children; ADR-058 D2), the row whose URL equals active marked aria-current="page" and no other; mint page_shell(title, active, content: Markup) -> String = page_head(title) + { render_viewer_nav(active) OUTSIDE +
(content)
} (ADR-058 D1/D6). SSOT co-location (architect's call, ADR-058 D2 / feature-delta [D4]): move LANDING_HUB_SURFACES from landing.rs to common.rs so render_viewer_nav consumes it in-module (avoids a common->landing back-edge); landing.rs references common::LANDING_HUB_SURFACES for its (still-present-this-step) inline hub. SURFACE RENDERERS (crates/viewer-domain/src/{claims,peer_claims,detail,scrape,search,score,traversal,peers}.rs + landing.rs): each render_*_page KEEPS its signature and return type; internally it stops spelling out DOCTYPE/head/body and instead builds its surface body as Markup and returns page_shell(title, active, body) where active is the surface's compile-time URL const at the call site (MY_CLAIMS_URL, SEARCH_URL, ...; base-path const for query-bearing routes; ADR-058 D6). The render_*_fragment fns are UNCHANGED (they ride Shape::Fragment for the existing tab #view-panel + paging #claims-table swaps). render_landing routes through page_shell too (so the nav renders on / for NAV-INV-LandingViaNav) but its inline LANDING_HUB_SURFACES hub is LEFT IN PLACE this step (removed at 01-02). EFFECT SHELL (crates/adapter-http-viewer/src/lib.rs): extend Shape::from_request with ONE prior condition -- HX-Boosted present -> FullPage (boosted nav returns the full page so the client hx-selects #viewer-main), else HX-Request present -> Fragment (existing tab/paging swaps UNCHANGED), else -> FullPage (direct / no-JS); route render_error (the 404 full page) through page_shell (active = "") so the nav is present for navigational recovery (ADR-058 D6). Outcome: the operator loads EVERY one of the 8 viewer routes (/ + the 7 inner surfaces) and, on each, sees the SAME persistent left nav listing all surfaces from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES as plain links wrapped around a #viewer-main content region, with the current surface marked aria-current="page" (the / landing carries no active marker). RED today: no
, no
, no aria-current="page", and no HX-Boosted arm exist. BUILD-BEFORE-RUN: the AT spawns the real openlore ui bin over HTTP; the run must cargo build --bin openlore before executing so ViewerServer::start spawns the CURRENT viewer. PARADIGM (functional Rust, ADR-007): the pure-core render fns are total functions of (title, active, content) / (active) with zero I/O; the header-unaware shape decision lives in the effect shell. Unit-layer coverage is a proptest over render_viewer_nav across the 8-surface x active-key space (exactly-one aria-current for a valid active key; every surface a plain ; the item set == LANDING_HUB_SURFACES); the acceptance scenarios are layer-3 subprocess + real-I/O EXAMPLES (Mandate 9/11; Tier B state-machine PBT NOT warranted -- a fixed 8-surface render, not a chained journey, Mandate 10).
acceptance_criteria: NAV-1 (AC-001.1/001.2, WS): with the real openlore ui viewer over a store seeded with the operator's own claims (via the real claim add verb), a GET of EVERY one of the 8 routes (/ /claims /peer-claims /search /score /project /philosophy /peers) returns 200 and a COMPLETE full page carrying the persistent left nav (id="viewer-nav") wrapped around the outer content region (id="viewer-main"), listing all surfaces from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES as plain <a href> links, and -- on every route that IS a nav item -- marking exactly that surface aria-current="page" (the / landing carries no active marker).

NAV-2 (AC-001.1 / KPI-NAV-1): every one of the 8 routes exposes the persistent nav container (id="viewer-nav") wrapping the inner item list (id="viewer-nav-items"), each listing all surfaces from the LANDING_HUB_SURFACES SSOT -- 100% nav reach.

NAV-5 (AC-001.4 / KPI-NAV-3): with JS off (a plain GET, no HX-Request), an inner surface (/peer-claims) still renders the nav on a COMPLETE full page with every surface a working plain <a href> full-page link (not an hx-get-only affordance).

The pure core (viewer-domain) mints VIEWER_MAIN_ID + render_viewer_nav(active) + page_shell(title, active, content); every render_*_page (and render_error) routes its body through page_shell with active = the surface's compile-time URL const; the render_*_fragment fns are byte-unchanged (tab #view-panel + paging #claims-table swaps intact); LANDING_HUB_SURFACES is co-located in common.rs as the single item source.

The effect shell (adapter-http-viewer) extends Shape::from_request with the prior arm HX-Boosted -> FullPage (boosted nav returns the full page for hx-select="#viewer-main"); the existing tab/paging hx-gets (HX-Request only) are UNCHANGED; render_error (404) routes through page_shell.

viewer-domain stays pure (the nav is plain <a href> links only -- no form/button/mutating control, no key, no new route/variant/crate); cargo xtask check-arch passes (workspace stays 21 members; pure-render + one adapter fork line); the openlore bin is built before the AT runs.
steps.mutation_note: Adds the load-bearing pure-core surface (VIEWER_MAIN_ID + render_viewer_nav + page_shell) and the Shape::from_request HX-Boosted -> FullPage fork. Mutation-test render_viewer_nav via a proptest over the pure fn (exactly-one aria-current for a valid active key; a NON-member active key yields zero markers; every surface emitted as a plain <a href>; the item set is exactly LANDING_HUB_SURFACES -- dropping/duplicating a row is caught) and the Shape fork (HX-Boosted -> FullPage without disturbing the HX-Request -> Fragment arm; swapping/eliding either arm is caught).
criteria: NAV-1 (AC-001.1/001.2, WS): with the real openlore ui viewer over a store seeded with the operator's own claims (via the real claim add verb), a GET of EVERY one of the 8 routes (/ /claims /peer-claims /search /score /project /philosophy /peers) returns 200 and a COMPLETE full page carrying the persistent left nav (id="viewer-nav") wrapped around the outer content region (id="viewer-main"), listing all surfaces from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES as plain <a href> links, and -- on every route that IS a nav item -- marking exactly that surface aria-current="page" (the / landing carries no active marker).

NAV-2 (AC-001.1 / KPI-NAV-1): every one of the 8 routes exposes the persistent nav container (id="viewer-nav") wrapping the inner item list (id="viewer-nav-items"), each listing all surfaces from the LANDING_HUB_SURFACES SSOT -- 100% nav reach.

NAV-5 (AC-001.4 / KPI-NAV-3): with JS off (a plain GET, no HX-Request), an inner surface (/peer-claims) still renders the nav on a COMPLETE full page with every surface a working plain <a href> full-page link (not an hx-get-only affordance).

The pure core (viewer-domain) mints VIEWER_MAIN_ID + render_viewer_nav(active) + page_shell(title, active, content); every render_*_page (and render_error) routes its body through page_shell with active = the surface's compile-time URL const; the render_*_fragment fns are byte-unchanged (tab #view-panel + paging #claims-table swaps intact); LANDING_HUB_SURFACES is co-located in common.rs as the single item source.

The effect shell (adapter-http-viewer) extends Shape::from_request with the prior arm HX-Boosted -> FullPage (boosted nav returns the full page for hx-select="#viewer-main"); the existing tab/paging hx-gets (HX-Request only) are UNCHANGED; render_error (404) routes through page_shell.

viewer-domain stays pure (the nav is plain <a href> links only -- no form/button/mutating control, no key, no new route/variant/crate); cargo xtask check-arch passes (workspace stays 21 members; pure-render + one adapter fork line); the openlore bin is built before the AT runs.

steps.name: Landing-hub migration + single source: LANDING_HUB_SURFACES is the sole nav item source, the inline hub is removed, slice-17 assertions re-baselined
steps.description: Complete the single-source guarantee of US-NAV-001 / AC-001.3 (NAV-4) and the ADR-058 Migration -- a direct consequence of the 01-01 shell. At 01-01 the persistent nav renders the surfaces on ALL routes via page_shell, but render_landing STILL carries the slice-17 inline LANDING_HUB_SURFACES hub, so the landing page briefly holds TWO surface lists. This step removes the inline hub from render_landing's body (the landing content region keeps only its store summary; ADR-058 D2/Migration), so LANDING_HUB_SURFACES is the GENUINELY single source read by exactly one renderer (render_viewer_nav) -- no second, driftable literal list. Re-baseline the slice-17 landing acceptance assertions (e.g. landing_hub_links_all_eight_surfaces_via_url_consts): the 'all 8 surfaces linked via URL consts' guarantee becomes an assertion against the persistent nav and now holds on ALL 8 routes, not just / (a STRENGTHENING -- broader coverage, guarded by the single-source test; ADR-058 Migration). No slice-17 behavior is lost: every link the landing hub offered is still offered (now from everywhere); the landing read-only + offline golds still hold (the nav is plain links). NAV-4 pins that an INNER surface (/score) sources the nav from the SAME LANDING_HUB_SURFACES set -- all 8 surfaces, and NO deep/parameterized route (/claims/{cid}, ?contributor=, ?subject=, ?object=) as a top-level nav item. RED cause today: /score renders no id="viewer-nav" (single back-link nav only). PARADIGM (functional Rust): the single-source invariant (the rendered nav item set is byte-derived from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES with no second literal) is a structural property pinned by the 01-01 render proptest (item set == LANDING_HUB_SURFACES); NAV-4 is a layer-3 subprocess EXAMPLE over /score. Migration edits are a mechanical hub removal + a test re-baseline (the full-page unit tests pin the landing result).
acceptance_criteria: NAV-4 (AC-001.3): with the operator on an inner surface (/score), GET /score returns 200 and renders the persistent nav (id="viewer-nav") listing ALL 8 surfaces from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES, and NO deep/parameterized route appears as a top-level nav item (the single source is the top-level SSOT, never a hand-rolled list that could add drill routes).

The inline LANDING_HUB_SURFACES hub is removed from render_landing's body (the landing content region keeps only its store summary); LANDING_HUB_SURFACES is now read by exactly one renderer (render_viewer_nav) -- a single, non-driftable source (AC-001.3).

The slice-17 landing-hub acceptance assertions are re-baselined to the persistent nav (a strengthening -- the 'all 8 surfaces via URL consts' guarantee now holds on all 8 routes, not just /); no slice-17 link is lost.
cargo xtask check-arch still passes (workspace stays 21 members; no new route/crate).
steps.mutation_note: The single-source derivation (the nav item set is byte-derived from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES, no second literal) is pinned by the 01-01 render proptest (item set == LANDING_HUB_SURFACES; a dropped or added row is caught). NAV-4 is the layer-3 example backstop that an inner surface (/score) renders the full SSOT set and no deep route as a top-level item.
criteria: NAV-4 (AC-001.3): with the operator on an inner surface (/score), GET /score returns 200 and renders the persistent nav (id="viewer-nav") listing ALL 8 surfaces from LANDING_HUB_SURFACES, and NO deep/parameterized route appears as a top-level nav item (the single source is the top-level SSOT, never a hand-rolled list that could add drill routes).

The inline LANDING_HUB_SURFACES hub is removed from render_landing's body (the landing content region keeps only its store summary); LANDING_HUB_SURFACES is now read by exactly one renderer (render_viewer_nav) -- a single, non-driftable source (AC-001.3).

The slice-17 landing-hub acceptance assertions are re-baselined to the persistent nav (a strengthening -- the 'all 8 surfaces via URL consts' guarantee now holds on all 8 routes, not just /); no slice-17 link is lost.
cargo xtask check-arch still passes (workspace stays 21 members; no new route/crate).

phases.name: The nav stays open across navigation (OOB active-marker swap, hx-boost history, byte-parity, no-regression)

steps.name: Out-of-band active-marker update on a boosted swap + hx-boost history attributes: the nav container persists while the marker updates in place
steps.description:
Resolve the US-NAV-002 persistence outcome (AC-002.1 refined + 002.3 + 002.2; NAV-6 + NAV-3 + NAV-7) via the ADR-058 D5 out-of-band mechanism. At 01-01 a boosted GET already returns the full page (the Shape fork) so the client can hx-select="#viewer-main", but the nav (OUTSIDE #viewer-main) would be left with a STALE active marker after a content-only swap. This step adds the OOB active-marker update WITHOUT re-flashing/reloading the nav. PURE CORE (crates/viewer-domain/src/common.rs): mint render_viewer_nav_oob(active: &str) -> Markup = JUST the bare
    ...active-updated links...
sibling (the same links as the in-shell list, active row marked aria-current="page"). EFFECT SHELL (crates/adapter-http-viewer/src/lib.rs): on a BOOSTED response only (HX-Boosted present), append render_viewer_nav_oob(active) to the full-page string at body-end (after ); direct / no-JS loads emit NEITHER the OOB copy NOR any hx-swap-oob. htmx processes the OOB content independently of hx-select, replacing ONLY the inner
    's children -- so the
    CONTAINER persists (never torn down, no scroll-reset, no flash) while the active marker updates in place (AC-002.1 refined + AC-002.3). NAV-7 confirms the in-shell nav already carries hx-boost="true" + hx-target="#viewer-main" (minted at 01-01) -- the mechanism htmx uses to push the surface URL into the address bar and make Back/forward work (AC-002.2). RED cause today: no HX-Boosted -> FullPage fork produced the full page (fixed 01-01), and no OOB hx-swap-oob nav-items are appended (fixed here); a boosted GET currently returns the bare fragment. PARADIGM (functional Rust): render_viewer_nav_oob is a pure total fn of active; its output (bare
      with hx-swap-oob, active row marked) is pinned by a unit proptest over the 8-surface active-key space; the effect-shell choice 'append OOB iff boosted' is verified by the layer-3 boosted vs full-page EXAMPLE scenarios (NAV-6/NAV-3 assert the OOB present on boosted and NAV-9's full/no-JS path asserts no OOB leaks -- confirmed at 02-02).
acceptance_criteria: NAV-6 (AC-002.1 refined / KPI-NAV-2): a boosted GET /score (HX-Request + HX-Boosted) returns 200 and a COMPLETE full page carrying the outer content region (id="viewer-main") for hx-select AND the appended out-of-band nav-items list (id="viewer-nav-items" with hx-swap-oob="innerHTML") -- so htmx swaps ONLY #viewer-main while the <nav id="viewer-nav"> container persists (not re-fetched or torn down).

NAV-3 (AC-001.2 + AC-002.3): on the full page for /claims exactly ONE nav item is marked aria-current="page"; after a boosted navigation to /peer-claims the response's OOB nav-items list carries the aria-current marker for the newly-active surface (the marker updates in place, previously-active no longer marked).

NAV-7 (AC-002.2): the persistent nav carries hx-boost="true" targeting/selecting #viewer-main (hx-target="#viewer-main") -- the htmx mechanism that pushes the navigated surface URL into the address bar and makes browser Back/forward return to the prior surface's content.

render_viewer_nav_oob(active) is minted in the pure core (the bare OOB <ul>); the effect shell appends it to the full-page string for boosted responses ONLY; direct / no-JS loads emit neither the OOB copy nor any hx-swap-oob (progressive enhancement unaffected).
The two existing swaps (#view-panel tab, #claims-table paging) are byte-unchanged (they never set HX-Boosted, so never hit the OOB path); cargo xtask check-arch still passes (21 members).
steps.mutation_note: The OOB copy is the load-bearing persistence machinery. Mutation-test render_viewer_nav_oob via a proptest (emits exactly the <ul id="viewer-nav-items" hx-swap-oob="innerHTML"> with the active row marked aria-current for a valid key; dropping the hx-swap-oob attr or the marker is caught) and the effect-shell append condition (OOB appended iff HX-Boosted -- appending it on a non-boosted response, or omitting it on a boosted one, is caught by NAV-6/NAV-3 vs the 02-02 no-regression path).
criteria: NAV-6 (AC-002.1 refined / KPI-NAV-2): a boosted GET /score (HX-Request + HX-Boosted) returns 200 and a COMPLETE full page carrying the outer content region (id="viewer-main") for hx-select AND the appended out-of-band nav-items list (id="viewer-nav-items" with hx-swap-oob="innerHTML") -- so htmx swaps ONLY #viewer-main while the <nav id="viewer-nav"> container persists (not re-fetched or torn down).

NAV-3 (AC-001.2 + AC-002.3): on the full page for /claims exactly ONE nav item is marked aria-current="page"; after a boosted navigation to /peer-claims the response's OOB nav-items list carries the aria-current marker for the newly-active surface (the marker updates in place, previously-active no longer marked).

NAV-7 (AC-002.2): the persistent nav carries hx-boost="true" targeting/selecting #viewer-main (hx-target="#viewer-main") -- the htmx mechanism that pushes the navigated surface URL into the address bar and makes browser Back/forward return to the prior surface's content.

render_viewer_nav_oob(active) is minted in the pure core (the bare OOB <ul>); the effect shell appends it to the full-page string for boosted responses ONLY; direct / no-JS loads emit neither the OOB copy nor any hx-swap-oob (progressive enhancement unaffected).
The two existing swaps (#view-panel tab, #claims-table paging) are byte-unchanged (they never set HX-Boosted, so never hit the OOB path); cargo xtask check-arch still passes (21 members).

steps.name: Byte-parity and no-regression confirmation: the boosted #viewer-main equals the full-page region, and prior surface content is unaffected except the added nav
steps.description: Confirm the two structural guarantees of US-NAV-002 (AC-002.4 parity + AC-002.5 no-regression; NAV-8 + NAV-9) that the 01-01 (page_shell + Shape fork) and 02-01 (OOB) work make TRUE by construction. (NAV-8, parity / KPI-NAV-4 / I-HX-5) Because a boosted response IS the full page and hx-select="#viewer-main" extracts exactly the full page's content region, the boosted #viewer-main inner region is byte-identical to the plain full-page #viewer-main region -- ONE render path per surface, parity that cannot diverge by luck. The behavioral proxy: fetch /claims boosted and plain, both carry #viewer-main, and the boosted #viewer-main region equals the full-page region byte-for-byte. Note the OOB <ul> is appended AFTER </main>, so it is OUTSIDE the extracted #viewer-main region and cannot perturb parity. (NAV-9, no-regression / KPI-NAV-4) The no-JS full-page render of a surface is byte-unaffected by this feature EXCEPT for the added nav region: /claims's pre-existing content (its read-only notice + OpenLore chrome) still renders intact, AND the persistent nav + #viewer-main are now additionally present -- the feature only ADDS the nav region, it does not rewrite the surface. This step is largely CONFIRMATORY (the parity + no-regression machinery lands at 01-01/02-01); it pins the invariants as their own guardrails and requires no new production change beyond what those steps deliver. PARADIGM (functional Rust): parity is a both-shapes-equal subprocess assertion over the SAME rendered surface (a wiring check, not a quantified-input property) -- EXEMPT FROM PBT at this layer; the compensating unit property is the render-path identity pinned by the 01-01 render proptest (one page_shell path per surface). NAV-8/NAV-9 are layer-3 subprocess + real-I/O EXAMPLES.
acceptance_criteria: NAV-8 (AC-002.4 / KPI-NAV-4 / I-HX-5): for /claims, both the boosted and the plain full-page responses return 200 and carry #viewer-main, and the boosted #viewer-main inner region is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the full-page #viewer-main region (parity by construction -- the boosted response IS the full page; hx-select cannot diverge from it; the appended OOB <ul> is outside the region).

NAV-9 (AC-002.5): the no-JS full page for /claims returns 200 as a COMPLETE full page; its pre-existing content survives intact (the read-only notice + the OpenLore chrome are byte-present as before) AND the persistent nav (id="viewer-nav") wrapping #viewer-main is now additionally present -- the ONLY change is the ADDED nav region.
No new production change is required beyond 01-01/02-01 (this step confirms the parity + no-regression invariants); cargo xtask check-arch still passes (21 members).
steps.mutation_note: Parity is by construction (the boosted response IS the full page; hx-select extracts the same #viewer-main; the OOB <ul> is appended outside the region). The mutation backstop is the 01-01 render proptest (one page_shell render path per surface) -- any second boosted-content code path that could drift would break the byte-identical region assertion. NAV-9's no-regression anchor (pre-existing content survives + only the nav is added) is pinned by the same single render path.
criteria: NAV-8 (AC-002.4 / KPI-NAV-4 / I-HX-5): for /claims, both the boosted and the plain full-page responses return 200 and carry #viewer-main, and the boosted #viewer-main inner region is BYTE-IDENTICAL to the full-page #viewer-main region (parity by construction -- the boosted response IS the full page; hx-select cannot diverge from it; the appended OOB <ul> is outside the region).

NAV-9 (AC-002.5): the no-JS full page for /claims returns 200 as a COMPLETE full page; its pre-existing content survives intact (the read-only notice + the OpenLore chrome are byte-present as before) AND the persistent nav (id="viewer-nav") wrapping #viewer-main is now additionally present -- the ONLY change is the ADDED nav region.
No new production change is required beyond 01-01/02-01 (this step confirms the parity + no-regression invariants); cargo xtask check-arch still passes (21 members).

steps.name: Gold: the six persistent-nav guardrail invariants (no control, offline/vendored asset, no-JS every route, single-source, landing no-regression, landing-via-nav)
steps.description: The six cross-cutting GOLD guardrails (NAV-INV-*) that must hold over the WHOLE persistent-nav surface -- the BEHAVIORAL layer of the ADR-058 D4 enforcement (the pure-core render proptests are the unit layer; xtask check-arch's 21-member / no-new-crate/route check is the structural layer). They flip GREEN off the 01-01 shell + 01-02 migration + 02-01 OOB work. (NAV-INV-NoControl, I-VIEW-1/3 / AC-001.5, CARDINAL) over /claims in BOTH shapes (full page + boosted), the NAV REGION (scoped id="viewer-nav"..</nav>, so /search and /scrape content-region forms do not false-positive) carries ONLY plain <a href> links -- NO <form>, <button>, or mutating hx-post/hx-put/hx-delete; the viewer holds no key and hx-boost issues GETs only. (NAV-INV-Offline, I-HX-2 / AC-001.5, GREEN-today) the surface references NO external CDN host and the hx-boost/hx-select capability rides the ALREADY-vendored /static/htmx.min.js (served locally, JS content-type) -- no new asset; must STAY green after the migration. (NAV-INV-NoJs, I-HX-1/4 / AC-001.4 / KPI-NAV-3) on EVERY one of the 8 routes the no-JS full page renders the nav with all 8 surfaces as working plain <a href> links. (NAV-INV-SingleSource, AC-001.3) the nav on an inner surface (/peer-claims) lists EXACTLY the LANDING_HUB_SURFACES set and no deep/parameterized route -- no second, driftable list. (NAV-INV-LandingNoRegression, ADR-058 Migration / KPI-NAV-4, GREEN-today) GET / STILL links all 8 surfaces (the slice-17 hub behavior is not lost -- DELIVER moved the links into the nav without dropping any); must STAY green. (NAV-INV-LandingViaNav, ADR-058 Migration) GET / now carries id="viewer-nav" -- the landing's 8 surface links come FROM the persistent nav (served through the shared page_shell + render_viewer_nav path, on / AND every other route). RED cause today: the nav chrome is absent (no id="viewer-nav"/#viewer-main) for the four RED golds; the Offline + LandingNoRegression golds are GREEN-today guardrails that must not regress. PARADIGM (functional Rust): these guardrails are layer-3 subprocess EXAMPLE golds tagged @property (universal invariants for the reader + crafter); the generative exploration of the pure render_viewer_nav / render_viewer_nav_oob / page_shell core over the 8-surface x active-key space is the layer-1/2 proptest concern landed in 01-01/02-01; these golds verify the OBSERVABLE surface end-to-end in both shapes.
acceptance_criteria: NAV-INV-NoControl (I-VIEW-1/3 / AC-001.5, CARDINAL): over /claims in BOTH shapes (full page + boosted), the nav region (scoped id="viewer-nav"..</nav>) carries NO executable/mutating control -- no <form>, <button>, hx-post, hx-put, or hx-delete -- only plain <a href> links; the viewer holds no key and hx-boost issues GETs only.

NAV-INV-Offline (I-HX-2 / AC-001.5, GREEN-today): the surface references NO external CDN host and the vendored /static/htmx.min.js serves locally (200, JS content-type) -- the offline foundation hx-boost/hx-select ride; stays green after the migration.
NAV-INV-NoJs (I-HX-1/4 / AC-001.4 / KPI-NAV-3): on EVERY one of the 8 routes, the no-JS full page renders the nav (id="viewer-nav") with all 8 surfaces as working plain <a href> links.

NAV-INV-SingleSource (AC-001.3): the nav on an inner surface (/peer-claims) lists EXACTLY the LANDING_HUB_SURFACES set and no deep/parameterized route as a top-level item -- one source, no second list.
NAV-INV-LandingNoRegression (ADR-058 Migration / KPI-NAV-4, GREEN-today): GET / STILL links all 8 surfaces (no link lost when the hub moved into the persistent nav).

NAV-INV-LandingViaNav (ADR-058 Migration): GET / carries id="viewer-nav" -- the landing's 8 surface links come FROM the persistent nav, served through the shared page_shell + render_viewer_nav path (the migration actually happened).
steps.mutation_note: These six guardrails are the behavioral layer of the ADR-058 D4 enforcement; ensure mutation testing of the pure render core (render_viewer_nav: exactly-one aria-current + item set == LANDING_HUB_SURFACES + plain <a href> only; render_viewer_nav_oob: the OOB <ul> + marker; page_shell: one render path) is green from the 01-01/02-01 proptests so the gold guardrails are not the only line of defense. The Offline + LandingNoRegression golds are GREEN-today no-regression pins (they must not flip red when the migration lands).
criteria: NAV-INV-NoControl (I-VIEW-1/3 / AC-001.5, CARDINAL): over /claims in BOTH shapes (full page + boosted), the nav region (scoped id="viewer-nav"..</nav>) carries NO executable/mutating control -- no <form>, <button>, hx-post, hx-put, or hx-delete -- only plain <a href> links; the viewer holds no key and hx-boost issues GETs only.

NAV-INV-Offline (I-HX-2 / AC-001.5, GREEN-today): the surface references NO external CDN host and the vendored /static/htmx.min.js serves locally (200, JS content-type) -- the offline foundation hx-boost/hx-select ride; stays green after the migration.
NAV-INV-NoJs (I-HX-1/4 / AC-001.4 / KPI-NAV-3): on EVERY one of the 8 routes, the no-JS full page renders the nav (id="viewer-nav") with all 8 surfaces as working plain <a href> links.

NAV-INV-SingleSource (AC-001.3): the nav on an inner surface (/peer-claims) lists EXACTLY the LANDING_HUB_SURFACES set and no deep/parameterized route as a top-level item -- one source, no second list.
NAV-INV-LandingNoRegression (ADR-058 Migration / KPI-NAV-4, GREEN-today): GET / STILL links all 8 surfaces (no link lost when the hub moved into the persistent nav).

NAV-INV-LandingViaNav (ADR-058 Migration): GET / carries id="viewer-nav" -- the landing's 8 surface links come FROM the persistent nav, served through the shared page_shell + render_viewer_nav path (the migration actually happened).
